tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":57231,"title":"[Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable","body":"## Description

The `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only "Update analyzer properties" without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.

The underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.

This has caused customer confusion — see #57215.

## Requested Improvements

1. **API doc (`` / ``)**: Add a `` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.

2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the "About updating analyzers" section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.

## Updatable Properties

| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |
|---|---|
| `description` | **Yes** |
| `tags` | **Yes** |
| `models` | No — silently ignored |
| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `config` | No — silently ignored |
| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |
| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |
| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |

## Workaround

To change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.

## Related

- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`

The underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. ","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"chienyuanchang"},"labels":["Cognitive - Content Understanding"],"created_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z"},{"number":57212,"title":"[provisioning] clean up redundant `Directory.Build.props` for provisioning libraries","body":"Provisioning libraries now have been moved into each serivce's serivce directory.
But they all have a complicated Directory.Build.props, for example: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/datafactory/Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory/Directory.Build.props

We should find a place to store some common properties, so that this file can be simplified.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang"},"labels":["EngSys","Provisioning"],"created_at":"2026-03-18T09:42:25Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:42:25Z"},{"number":57205,"title":"[Provisioning] Implement a new serialization system for BicepTypes","body":"It would be nice if all the bicep types could use IPersistableModel to support serialization into bicep snippets and aspire RPCs.

More details will be updated here.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang"},"labels":["CodeGen","Provisioning"],"created_at":"2026-03-18T03:07:39Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:07:54Z"},{"number":57184,"title":"Explore TypeForwarding Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions","body":"## Problem

Individual SDK libraries (e.g., `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets`) need to provide `AddFooClient` DI extension methods that call `WithAzureCredential` to enable the full configuration and dependency injection experience described in #55491. Currently, `WithAzureCredential` and its supporting types (`ConfigurableCredential`, `ConfigurableCredentialCache`, `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions`) are defined in `Azure.Identity`. This means each SDK library would need to take a **new direct dependency on Azure.Identity** to offer these extensions — adding a dependency to every SDK package that doesn't currently have one.

## Proposed Approach to Explore

Since all SDK libraries already depend on `Azure.Core`, and `Azure.Identity` already depends on `Azure.Core`, we should explore whether we can use [TypeForwardedTo](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute) to make the relevant types available through `Azure.Core` without requiring a direct `Azure.Identity` dependency.

The high-level idea:
1. **Move** the types needed by `WithAzureCredential` from `Azure.Identity` into `Azure.Core`
2. **Add** `[assembly: TypeForwardedTo(...)]` attributes in `Azure.Identity` pointing to the new locations in `Azure.Core`
3. SDK libraries can then reference these types through their existing `Azure.Core` dependency
4. **Existing consumers of Azure.Identity are unaffected** — the runtime follows type forwarding transparently, so no breaking change occurs

This should not break any existing users of `Azure.Identity` since:
- `TypeForwardedTo` is a well-established .NET mechanism for moving types across assemblies
- `Azure.Core` is already in every SDK's dependency chain
- Existing compiled code referencing these types via `Azure.Identity` will be redirected at runtime

## Types That May Need to Move

Based on the current `WithAzureCredential` implementation in `Azure.Identity/src/ConfigurationExtensions.cs`, the following types are referenced:

| Type | Current Location | Notes |
|------|-----------------|-------|
| `ConfigurableCredential` | Azure.Identity (currently internal) | Extends `TokenCredential`; visibility may need adjustment |
| `ConfigurableCredentialCache` | Azure.Identity | Static caching layer for credential reuse |
| `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` | Azure.Identity | Options class for credential configuration |
| `WithAzureCredential` extensions | Azure.Identity (`ConfigurationExtensions`) | The extension methods on `ClientSettings` and `IClientBuilder` |

## Open Questions and Risks

- **Internal types**: `ConfigurableCredential` is currently `internal` — `TypeForwardedTo` only works with public types, so the API surface may need to be adjusted or an alternative abstraction introduced
- **Transitive dependency concerns**: `ConfigurableCredential` has deep dependencies on MSAL libraries, `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ChainedTokenCredential`, etc. — moving it directly to `Azure.Core` could pull in unwanted transitive dependencies unless the design is refactored (e.g., using an interface/abstraction in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity implements)
- **Alternative patterns**: Instead of moving concrete types, it may be more appropriate to define an abstraction or factory pattern in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity fulfills at runtime
- **Existing precedent**: `TypeForwardedTo` is rarely used in this repo today (only 1 instance found in `Azure.Messaging.EventGrid`), so this would establish a new pattern

## Goal

Enable SDK libraries to provide `AddFooClient` extension methods with full `WithAzureCredential` support **without adding a new direct dependency on Azure.Identity**. This issue tracks the investigation of TypeForwarding as the primary approach, but alternative solutions may emerge during exploration.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"m-nash"},"labels":["Client","Azure.Core","Azure.Identity"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-17T18:53:04Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:04:52Z"},{"number":57183,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> 
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"brandonh-msft"},"labels":["Service Attention","Client","customer-reported","needs-team-attention","AI Agents"],"created_at":"2026-03-17T18:52:04Z","updated_at":"2026-03-17T21:07:17Z"}],"totalCount":990,"pageInfo":{"hasNextPage":true,"hasPreviousPage":false,"startCursor":"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wMy0xOVQwMjozOTozOVrO9E_-pw==","endCursor":"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wMy0xN1QxODo1MjowNFrO89BWGQ=="}}

arguments.message: No action needed: Issue #57231 already has the label "Cognitive - Content Understanding" applied. Per triage precondition checks, issues with existing labels are skipped to avoid overriding prior triage work.
